title: What was Rei III thinking about Gendos glasses
display_title: What was Rei III thinking about Gendos glasses
wikitext:
[]: What was Rei III thinking about Gendo's glasses?             Quote Post by whisperwind777 » Tue Oct 31, 2017 11:47 am In NGE episode 23, when Rei III got back to her appartment, she took off the bandage, stood in front of the mirror for a while and then grasped Gendo's glasses with both her hands, was she trying to break the glasses? What was on her mind? Rei III must have realized something, or she wouldn't do such a thing since Rei II had been keeping the glasses better than she had been keeping herself. And then her tears fell and surprised herself, suggesting she didn't have all of Rei II's memories. But why she tried to destory the glasses? We know she finally broke them in EOE. Sorry if this has been discussed before, but the more I think about it, the more it scares me.

[]: Re: What was Rei III thinking about Gendo's glasses?             Quote Post by shadmere » Sun Dec 24, 2017 2:04 am She was upset because she could tell that the glasses meant something to her, but she couldn't remember what. She had an emotional connection to the glasses for, apparently, no reason. Her soul "remembered" the glasses as important, but her actual memories did not. The glasses proved that her existence was broken into pieces. That she didn't have control of her own life, or even over her own feelings. That she was alive, but only because she was a tool. She had no life of her own. To everyone else, she was "Rei." Even to the people who knew she was a clone, she was "Rei" brought back to life. But to her, she was a new person. Except she had a stranger's emotions inside her, and she couldn't do anything about them. That's why she hated the glasses. Because she cared about them for "no reason" and couldn't stop herself from it.

[]: Re: What was Rei III thinking about Gendo's glasses?             Quote Post by TheCarkolum » Sat Jan 06, 2018 11:22 am I always thought it was anger. She remembered (with her feelings) how being dependent on Gendo led her to death. That's why she cries. She feels useless, used and out of love for Gendo.

[]: Re: What was Rei III thinking about Gendo's glasses?             Quote Post by whisperwind777 » Sun Jan 14, 2018 7:28 am shadmere wrote: She was upset because she could tell that the glasses meant something to her, but she couldn't remember what. She had an emotional connection to the glasses for, apparently, no reason. Her soul "remembered" the glasses as important, but her actual memories did not. The glasses proved that her existence was broken into pieces. That she didn't have control of her own life, or even over her own feelings. That she was alive, but only because she was a tool. She had no life of her own. To everyone else, she was "Rei." Even to the people who knew she was a clone, she was "Rei" brought back to life. But to her, she was a new person. Except she had a stranger's emotions inside her, and she couldn't do anything about them. That's why she hated the glasses. Because she cared about them for "no reason" and couldn't stop herself from it.

[]: Re: What was Rei III thinking about Gendo's glasses?             Quote Post by whisperwind777 » Sun Jan 14, 2018 8:24 am TheCarkolum wrote: I always thought it was anger. She remembered (with her feelings) how being dependent on Gendo led her to death. That's why she cries. She feels useless, used and out of love for Gendo.

[]: Re: What was Rei III thinking about Gendo's glasses?             Quote Post by TheCarkolum » Sun Jan 14, 2018 9:53 am whisperwind777 wrote: I agree with you that it was anger, but not that it was because of her death. Rei was never afraid of death. And Shiji told her that Rei II sacrifices herself to save her.
